title: Public Keys & SSL 🌐
description: How HTTPS keeps your credit card safe.

slides.title: The HTTPS Lock 🌐
slides.content:
### Safe Web Browsing

When you see the padlock icon in your browser, you are using **HTTPS**. This is just HTTP wrapped in a layer of encryption (SSL/TLS).

*   **Goal**: Ensure that no one can 'eavesdrop' on your credit card info or passwords while they travel over the internet.

slides.title: Digital Certificates 📜
slides.content:
### Proving Identity

How do you know `google.com` is really Google? They have a **Digital Certificate** signed by a **Certificate Authority (CA)**.

Your browser trust these CAs implicitly. If a certificate is self-signed or from an untrusted source, your browser warns you: **'Your connection is not private'**.

quizData.question: What is the primary role of a Certificate Authority (CA)?

options.text: To verify the identity of a website and sign its digital certificate
To encrypt all emails by default

quizData.explanation: CAs act like a digital DMV, verifying that a domain owner is who they say they are.
